project_type: Terraform infrastructure project with Python Lambda functions for AWS Ground Station NOAA-20 demonstrator
build_system: Terraform CLI for infrastructure validation, Python for Lambda code
test_framework: terraform validate + terraform fmt for HCL, pytest for Python Lambda code
verification_instructions: 1. Run terraform fmt -check -recursive to verify formatting. 2. Run terraform init to download providers. 3. Run terraform validate to confirm syntactically valid configuration. 4. Run python3 -m py_compile on Lambda source files to verify Python syntax.
setup_instructions: 1. Install Terraform CLI. 2. Run terraform init in root module. 3. Ensure Python 3.9+ for Lambda development.
environment_constraints: Terraform is NOT pre-installed - must be installed. OPEN_INTERNET network mode allows full access to registry.terraform.io and PyPI. No AWS credentials available so cannot run terraform plan/apply - validation only.
contribution_requirements: Modular Terraform structure with reusable modules. All Lambda code in Python. Region validation against Ground Station supported regions.
key_patterns: Standard Terraform module structure: root module calls child modules. Variables with validation blocks. Outputs for ARNs and resource names. Python Lambda code packaged as zip archives.
directory_structure: Root: main.tf, variables.tf, outputs.tf, versions.tf, terraform.tfvars.example. modules/: one module per requirement area. lambdas/: Python source for Lambda functions.
